spec_match.rationale: The timeline and migration map accurately align with standard historical releases of the Attribution Reporting API (OT from Chrome 117,GA through Chrome 148,deprecation in 150,removal in 151).

interactive.rationale: Executes live capability testing immediately on-load, displaying which ARA surfaces are active, parsing the browser User Agent, and showing a contextual version header.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned perfectly with the parent index: timelines from Chrome 117 through 151+, shows active remaining surfaces, and contrasts them with a server-side map.

edge_cases.rationale: Safely handles Request constructor testing inside a try-catch block to prevent page-load script breakage.

references.rationale: Directly links to the parent index and target entries in see also.

design_system.rationale: Beautiful timeline styling utilizing logical left borders, rounded indicator bullets, consistent widths, and color variables.

openQuestions.title: Provide a live user-agent string override tester
openQuestions.detail: To make the page even more interactive, we could add a text input where users can paste a custom User Agent string (or choose historic Chrome versions from a select dropdown) to simulate how the timeline note would render and update for other developers' browser settings.

summary: A stellar chronological deprecation resource. By pairing an interactive version/surface probe with a release timeline and direct first-party equivalents mapping, it provides clear and actionable direction for developers migrating away from the API. Highly compliant with all guidelines.
